CREATE TABLE test_strings (s1 VARCHAR(255), s2 VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO test_strings VALUES ('bcd', 'bc'), ('abcdef', 'abc'), ('hello world', 'hello'), (NULL, 'test'), ('test', NULL), (NULL, NULL);
SELECT startswith(s1, 'test') FROM test_strings WHERE s2 IS NOT NULL;
SELECT startswith(s1, s2) FROM test_strings WHERE s1 IS NOT NULL AND s2 IS NOT NULL;
SELECT startswith(s1, 'b') AS starts_with_b, startswith(s2, 'he') AS starts_with_he FROM test_strings;
SELECT t1.s1, t2.s2, startswith(t1.s1, t2.s2) FROM test_strings t1 JOIN test_strings t2 ON t1.s1 != t2.s1;
SELECT CASE WHEN startswith(s1, s2) THEN 'Yes' ELSE 'No' END AS result FROM test_strings;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_strings WHERE startswith(s1, 'abc');
SELECT s1 FROM test_strings WHERE startswith(s1, (SELECT s2 FROM test_strings WHERE s1 = 'bcd'));
